signals.reasoning: The live SOL short is still retaining about 97% of peak unrealized PnL, so it has not become a cleanup exit yet. The new veto headline works against the short, though, so this is a tighter manage-it hold rather than a high-conviction hold.
BTC is stabilizing on the relief headline, which is exactly why fresh shorts are less attractive under veto. Best to stay flat outside the live position.
ETH is also leaning into the relief tape, making it a poor candidate for new bearish action while the event brake is active.
TST remains far too extended and volatile to trust through a live geopolitical headline flip. There is no need to force it.
STABLE still has some squeeze fuel, but the live headline regime and its already-improved structure make it a poor fresh decision while a position is open.
MERL is still just a rebound with no cleaner edge than the trade already on the book. Watch-only.
BRETT is constructive, but the live relief headline makes chasing recovery names a bad use of attention while managing a short.
STBL has improved enough that it no longer offers a clean bearish edge, but it is not a clean long either. Neutral is the disciplined answer.
WCT is too extended and too event-sensitive to touch here. There is no reason to add a second decision problem.
VVV still looks like recovery and short-covering rather than a clean trend continuation. It is not a better expression than simply managing SOL.
ZEREBRO remains noisy and high beta, which makes it a poor candidate under a live event-driven veto. Better to stand down.
